text:
Punters in a packedParispub went wild as they were joined by cyclist Nils Politt in the middle of the men’s road race as theOlympianpopped in for a toilet stop.

TheGermanrider revealed after the event that he was struggling with an upset stomach and he was forced to dash into the famous Cafe des 2 Moulin in Montmartre.

A remarkable video of the moment sees the cyclist being led through the busy bar, past drinkers with beers in hand.

The crowd obviously loved being up close and personal with the athlete as he returned to his bike and cycled off.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

Politt was not loving life so much, explaining the nightmare situation he found himself in.

‘Did not count how much time it took me,’ Politt said,’ viaFox Sports.

‘It was really warm, we were drinking a lot of water, ate a lot of gels and normally I don’t have problems. But today I had an upset stomach.’

La Dépêche du Midi journalist Arthur Tirat posted the video with the superb piece of French: ‘Pause pipi’.

Pause pipi au cÃ©lÃ¨bre CafÃ© des 2 Moulins pour Nils Politt#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/ybTxqwJqqB

Politt had looked in good shape early on in the road race, but tailed off and finished 70th, certainly not helped by his unexpected visit to the boozer.

Belgium’s Remco Evenepoel claimed gold, with the French pair of Valentin Madouas and Christophe Laporte taking silver and bronze respectively.

Britain’s gold medal hero from the men’s cross-country mountain biking, Tom Pidcock, was also in action, finishing 13th.

‘I didn’t have that extra punch. I was a bit sluggish and slow, and so I was never really in the proper race,’ said Pidcock.

‘Coming out of the mountain biking, I didn’t want any pressure on this race. I said I would go on the calls and talk about it, but my focus was mountain biking. To be honest, my expectations were to be in the race and if I’d got a medal that would be awesome.’

title: Olympic cyclist forced to dash into rowdy pub for toilet break mid-race | Metro News
description: Punters in a Paris pub went wild as they were joined by cyclist Nils Politt as the 2024 Olympian popped in for a toilet stop mid-race.
